Also, the use of o_ioepoch for its original purpose was only dropped in commit d5d5b349f 2.7.50, so it would likely cause this unrelated value from old clients to be used instead of the XID expected from new clients.

Whatever fix is used for this bug, it makes sense to mark o_ioepoch as obsolete in 2.8 so we know this for the future.

Patch Set 10: Code-Review+1

my only (though big) concern is that there is no test to ensure the patch works correctly and the feature isn't broken at some point.

Patch Set 10:

Could you fix the way export_last_xid is updated in lustre/target/tgt_handler.c, since the export_last_xid should always increase ?

at line 673
-       if (last_xid != 0)
-               req->rq_export->exp_last_xid = last_xid;
+       if (last_xid > req->rq_export->exp_last_xid)
+               req->rq_export->exp_last_xid = last_xid;

Updating this has revealed unexpected failure in patch http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/15661/.

Patch Set 10:

Gregoire, I think the 'bug' you mentioned is actually because the LASSERT() itself isn't correct, it was a replay request triggered that LASSERT. For replay requests, they may carry a last_xid which smaller than the last_xid carried by connect request.

Patch Set 19:

Two problems are addressed in this version:

1. Don't set last_xid for the DISCONNECT RPC, because client will abort inflgiht RPC before sending DISCONNECT, so DISCONNECT may carry a untrustworthy last_xid to server. I think it's the root cause of the failures observed by Oleg.

2. Handle resend on EINPROGRESS resend request properly. (request got resend for EINPROGRESS error, then it timedout, and we do a normal resend on it).

commit.subject: LU-5951 ptlrpc: track unreplied requests
commit.message:
LU-5951 ptlrpc: track unreplied requests

The request xid was used to make sure the ost object timestamps
being updated by the out of order setattr/punch/write requests
properly. However, this mechanism is broken by the multiple rcvd
slot feature, where we deferred the xid assignment from request
packing to request sending.

This patch moved back the xid assignment to request packing, and
the manner of finding lowest unreplied xid is changed from scan
sending & delay list to scan a unreplied requests list.

This patch also skipped packing the known replied XID in connect
and disconnect request, so that we can make sure the known replied
XID is increased only on both server & client side.
